RESPONSIVE BEHAVIOR\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nThe journal must remain convincing on:\r\n- Large desktop displays\r\n- Standard laptop displays\r\n- Narrow desktop windows\r\n- Tablet-sized layouts\r\n- Mobile layouts where supported\r\n\r\nDo not simply shrink the full desktop journal until the text becomes microscopic.\r\n\r\nFor smaller viewports:\r\n- Preserve correct left/right physical page identity\r\n- Reframe or scale the book intelligently\r\n- Avoid clipping page turns\r\n- Keep the gutter centered\r\n- Ensure objective cards and chapter navigation do not cover crucial journal content\r\n- Allow an appropriate single-page mobile mode if the existing responsive design requires it\r\n- Maintain cinematic transitions without excessive GPU load\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n16. PERFORMANCE AND RENDERING QUALITY\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nAvoid:\r\n- Excessive forced reflow\r\n- Constant React state updates per animation frame\r\n- Huge uncompressed textures\r\n- Unbounded particle creation\r\n- Permanent `will-change` on every element\r\n- Multiple duplicate full-resolution book layers\r\n- Event listeners that accumulate after replays\r\n- Animation intervals that continue after unmount\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n- CSS transforms\r\n- Opacity\r\n- GPU-friendly compositing\r\n- Carefully scoped `will-change`\r\n- RequestAnimationFrame where truly needed\r\n- Memoized page content where appropriate\r\n- Proper cleanup\r\n\r\nInspect performance while:\r\n- Opening the journal\r\n- Breaking the seal\r\n- Turning pages\r\n- Replaying the introduction\r\n- Resizing the viewport\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n18. NO VISUAL CHEATING DURING PAGE FLIPS\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nPerform deliberate frame-by-frame inspection at:\r\n- 0%\r\n- 10%\r\n- 25%\r\n- 50%\r\n- 75%\r\n- 90%\r\n- 100%\r\n\r\nAt every stage verify:\r\n\r\n- The turning page is above the correct underlying page.\r\n- The binding does not show through opaque parchment.\r\n- The brown rectangular artifact is absent.\r\n- The page backside is correctly rendered.\r\n- The page edge is not razor straight.\r\n- The page does not clip through the cover.\r\n- Shadows match the current curvature.\r\n- Text does not appear backward or duplicated incorrectly.\r\n- Page corners remain physically oriented.\r\n- The left page never becomes a right-page duplicate.\r\n- No temporary layer remains after completion.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n19. STATE AND PROGRESS SAFETY\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nThis animation reconstruction must not break story progression.\r\n\r\nPreserve:\r\n- Current tale state\r\n- Chapter lock/unlock state\r\n- Captain-controlled progression\r\n- Current objective\r\n- Journal page content\r\n- Story block rendering\r\n- Saved player progress\r\n- Reload behavior\r\n- Existing routes\r\n- Admin/editor integration\r\n- Future vision-helper compatibility\r\n\r\nAnimation state must be separate from permanent story state.\r\n\r\nFor example:\r\n- A seal being visually broken must not incorrectly unlock a chapter.\r\n- Replaying the opening must not reset progress.\r\n- Reloading should not corrupt the tale state.\r\n- Development animation testing must not alter saved progression.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n20. INITIAL LOAD AND HYDRATION SAFETY\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nPrevent all initial-load flashes.\r\n\r\nBefore showing the entry screen:\r\n- Resolve the necessary tale state.\r\n- Determine whether this is a fresh cinematic entry, a resumed session, or a replay request.\r\n- Apply initial book pose before the first visible frame.\r\n- Ensure fonts and critical book assets are ready enough to avoid layout jumps.\r\n- Display a thematically appropriate loading state if necessary.\r\n\r\nDo not render the open journal for one frame and then hide it.\r\n\r\nDo not render a default page and then replace it after hydration.\r\n\r\nThe first visible composition must already be valid.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n21. VISUAL ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nThe task is not complete until all of the following are true:\r\n\r\nBOOK CONSTRUCTION:\r\n- The left page looks physically like a left page.\r\n- The right page looks physically like a right page.\r\n- Outer corners and gutter corners are correctly oriented.\r\n- The page stack has believable thickness.\r\n- The gutter appears recessed.\r\n- The binding spans the book naturally.\r\n- The binding never appears through pages.\r\n\r\nARTIFACT REMOVAL:\r\n- The brown box is completely gone.\r\n- No replacement placeholder remains.\r\n- No detached latch shape appears over open pages.\r\n\r\nWEAR:\r\n- Page edges are irregular and worn.\r\n- Corners are subtly aged.\r\n- The pages do not look like perfect digital rectangles.\r\n- Texture repetition is not obvious.\r\n\r\nPAGE FLIPS:\r\n- Pages bow and curl.\r\n- Flip shadows move dynamically.\r\n- The turning edge is not perfectly straight.\r\n- Pages settle naturally.\r\n- No clipping or layer leaks occur.\r\n\r\nOPENING:\r\n- The journal begins closed.\r\n- The latch is attached and functional.\r\n- The latch unlocks before the cover opens.\r\n- The cover opens with weight.\r\n- The sealed page appears after opening.\r\n- The seal fractures organically.\r\n- Wax fragments behave physically.\r\n- The journal settles into the active state.\r\n\r\nTRANSITIONS:\r\n- â€œOpen the journalâ€ does not flash directly to the main page.\r\n- The introduction does not flash directly to the main page.\r\n- The book remains visually continuous between scenes.\r\n- Navigation and objective UI enter after the journal settles.\r\n- There are no blank frames or one-frame layout swaps.\r\n\r\nREPLAY:\r\n- Replaying works repeatedly without duplicated objects, audio, or stale layers.\r\n- Reloading produces the correct entry state.\r\n- Development tests do not alter story progress.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n22. REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION PROCESS\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nBefore editing:\r\n\r\n1. Inspect the current book, page, binding, seal, latch, entry screen, introduction, and main journal components.\r\n2. Identify which components are duplicated.\r\n3. Identify all fixed overlays and high-z-index elements.\r\n4. Find the source of the brown rectangle.\r\n5. Trace the current opening flow.\r\n6. Trace the current introduction completion flow.\r\n7. Document the current scene transitions and state variables.\r\n8. Identify whether the flashes come from:\r\n   - Component replacement\r\n   - Route changes\r\n   - Hydration\r\n   - Default state\r\n   - Asset loading\r\n   - Uncoordinated timeouts\r\n   - React remounting\r\n   - CSS transitions being skipped\r\n9. Determine whether the existing page-turn system can support deforming pages.\r\n10. Refactor before layering more effects onto a structurally incorrect system.\r\n\r\nDuring implementation:\r\n\r\n1. Build the persistent journal stage.\r\n2. Correct left/right page geometry.\r\n3. Rebuild the gutter and binding layering.\r\n4. Remove the brown artifact.\r\n5. Add worn page edges and page-stack depth.\r\n6. Rebuild page-turn deformation.\r\n7. Build the closed cover and attached latch.\r\n8. Build the opening state machine.\r\n9. Rebuild the wax fracture animation.\r\n10. Connect introduction completion to the persistent main journal.\r\n11. Repair replay and test controls.\r\n12. Test all routes, states, and viewport sizes.\r\n\r\nAfter implementation:\r\n\r\n1. Run linting.\r\n2. Run type checking.\r\n3. Run unit and integration tests.\r\n4. Add tests for the scene state machine where practical.\r\n5. Test multiple consecutive replays.\r\n6. Test direct reload.\r\n7. Test fresh session.\r\n8. Test resumed tale.\r\n9. Test sound disabled.\r\n10. Test reduced motion.\r\n11. Test forward and backward page flips.\r\n12. Inspect animations in slow motion.\r\n13. Capture screenshots or recordings at critical animation stages if the development environment supports it.\r\n14. Verify no console errors or warnings.\r\n15. Verify no story state or persistence regressions.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n23. DO NOT DO THESE THINGS\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n- Hide structural problems with a dark overlay.\r\n- Use a generic fade between every scene.\r\n- Keep the binding above all pages.\r\n- Leave the latch mounted over the open journal.\r\n- Use straight lines as the final wax fracture effect.\r\n- Use two copies of the right-page component.\r\n- Use perfectly rectangular page edges.\r\n- Make page flips rigid.\r\n- Instantly replace the introduction with the main page.\r\n- Add unrelated new features.\r\n- Change story text without necessity.\r\n- Reset player progress.\r\n- Remove the cinematic animation system.\r\n- shorten all animations to generic UI timing.\r\n- claim completion without visually testing actual transitions.\r\n\r\n============================================================\r\n24. FINAL REPORT\r\n============================================================\r\n\r\nWhen complete, provide a detailed implementation report containing:\r\n\r\n1. Root causes found\r\n2. Components modified\r\n3. Components created\r\n4. Components removed\r\n5. How left and right page geometry now differ\r\n6. How binding occlusion is handled\r\n7. How page deformation is implemented\r\n8. How the latch is physically attached and animated\r\n9. How the wax seal fractures\r\n10. The scene state machine states and transition triggers\r\n11. How the introduction transitions into the persistent journal\r\n12. How reload and replay behavior work\r\n13. How story progress was protected\r\n14. Performance considerations\r\n15. Responsive behavior\r\n16. Reduced-motion behavior\r\n17. Tests performed\r\n18. Remaining limitations, if any\r\n19. Exact files changed\r\n\r\nDo not provide a vague â€œpolished animationsâ€ summary.\r\n\r\nThe finished experience should feel as though the player is opening an actual treasured captainâ€™s journal, not clicking through several web pages that happen to contain drawings of one.\r\n\n---WORKSPACE---\r\n\r\n\r\n    Directory: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\r\n\r\n\r\nMode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name                                                                 \r\n----                 -------------         ------ ----                                                                 \r\nd-----         7/17/2026   5:00 PM                forever-treasure-companion                                           \r\nd-----         7/16/2026   5:32 PM                phase3-clean-clone-validation                                        \r\nd-----         7/16/2026   5:55 PM                phase3-game-master-command-center                                    \r\nd-----         7/17/2026   2:26 PM                tmp                                                                  \r\n\n---GIT---\r\n\r\n\r\nfatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Always use Sites when the project contains `.openai/hosting.json`.\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Sites building\r\n\r\nBuild the complete requested site, validate it, then use `sites-hosting`\r\nunless the user explicitly asks to keep it local.\r\n\r\n## Communicate clearly\r\n\r\nAssume the user is a nontechnical knowledge worker. Talk about their site,\r\nchoices, progress, and results. Keep tools, commands, files, runtimes, browser\r\nsoftware, permissions, dependencies, source control, credentials, IDs, builds,\r\nand deployment internals out of user-facing messages unless the user asks or\r\nmust take action.\r\n\r\nUse no more than one short update for each user-visible phase: preparing design\r\noptions, building the selected option, and publishing. If a phase takes longer\r\nthan 60 seconds, give one plain-language update. Keep recoverable technical\r\nproblems private; say only that you hit a problem and are trying another method.\r\n\r\n## Choose the execution path\r\n\r\nUse the **one-shot fast path** only when all of these are true:\r\n\r\n- this is a new site in an empty or projectless workspace;\r\n- one route can satisfy the request;\r\n- the request does not require D1, R2, uploads, app-owned authentication,\r\n  external connectors, or browser UI QA; and\r\n- the normal deliverable is a private deployed URL.\r\n\r\nUse the **capability path** otherwise. This includes existing-site changes,\r\nmulti-route sites, persistent data, uploads, authentication, external data, and\r\nrequested browser testing.\r\n\r\n## Use imagery purposefully\r\n\r\nAvoid model-authored SVGs in finished sites, including inline SVG\r\nillustrations. Prefer strong typography, color, layout, CSS shapes, and existing\r\nicon components when imagery is unnecessary. When a site needs real imagery,\r\nprefer suitable images found through web image search. Use `imagegen` if and\r\nonly if original imagery is important and a suitable existing image is\r\nunavailable; generation adds latency, so keep it purposeful and limited.\r\n\r\n## Start new projects immediately\r\n\r\nFor a new site in an empty or projectless workspace, make setup the first task\r\naction. Run this plugin's root-level `scripts/init-site.sh` with `$PWD` as its\r\ntarget and retain the session until installation completes. Do not run a second\r\ninitializer.\r\n\r\nIn a visible foreground thread, wait for setup to finish, then immediately start\r\n`npm run dev` in a retained session. Use the exact Local URL printed by the\r\ndevelopment server and call `open_in_codex` once. Complete these startup steps\r\nbefore asking discovery questions, calling `request_user_input`, or showing a\r\ndesign picker. \"@/components/dev/AnimationTestButton\";\n\nexport function AccessGate({ campaignSlug }: { campaignSlug: string }) {\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\n  const setError(\"\");\n    const form = new FormData(event.currentTarget);\n    try {\n      await director.play(\"player-access\", {\n        root: root.current,\n        queue: false,\n        operation:  <main ref={root} className=\"access-scene\" data-motion-mode={mode}>\n      <div className=\"chart-room-table\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\n        <i className=\"table-compass\" />\n        <i line of fog at the chart-room edge\"\n        className=\"access-edge-fog\"\n      />\n      <div className=\"access-lantern\" data-scene-part=\"lantern\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\n        <i />\n        <b />\n      </div>\n      <section className=\"access-card\" data-scene-part=\"invitation\" data-gsap-owned aria-labelledby=\"invitation-title\">\n        <div <AnimationTestButton />\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n\n---JOURNAL WORKSPACE---\r\n\"use client\";\n\nimport { useMemo, useRef } from \"react\";\nimport type { MotionMode } from data-scene-part=\"ink-heading\" data-gsap-owned>\n          {String(payload.title ?? \"\")}\n        </h3>\n        <p className=\"ceremony-story\" data-scene-part=\"ink-story\" data-gsap-owned course</span>\n          <strong>{String(payload.objective ?? \"\")}</strong>\n        </div>\n        <blockquote data-scene-part=\"ink-riddle\" data-gsap-owned data-ink-copy>\n          {String(payload.riddle ?? \"\")}\n        </blockquote>\n        <svg className=\"ceremony-route\" viewBox=\"0 0 460 120\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\n          <path data-quill-path d=\"M18 86C130 12 270 24 438 82\" />\n          <path data-scene-part=\"route-path\" d=\"M18 86C130 12 270 24 438 82\" />\n        </svg>\n        <div className=\"ceremony-map-fog\" data-scene-part=\"map-fog\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n        <div className=\"ceremony-quill\" data-scene-part=\"quill\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n        <LottieEffect\n          asset={lottieAssets.inkBloom}\n          mode={mode}\n          label=\"Ink blooming and drying across the released page\"\n          className=\"ceremony-ink-bloom\"\n        />\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\r\n\n---PAGE FLIP BOOK---\r\n\"use client\";\n\nimport { forwardRef, useEffect, useImperativeHandle, useMemo, useRef, useState } from \"react\";\nimport type { PageFlip as PageFlipInstance } from \"page-flip\";\nimport type { MotionMode } from \"@/animation/core/animation-types\";\nimport { changeMountedMetric, recordAssetFailure } from \"@/animation/core/metrics\";\n\nexport type PageFlipBookHandle = {\n  next: () => void;\n  previous: () => void;\n  turnTo: (page: number) => void;\n  flipTo: (page: number) => void;\n  currentPage: () => number;\n  pageCount: () => number;\n  orientation: () => \"portrait\" | \"landscape\";\n};\n\nexport type FlipBookPage = {\n  id: string;\n  density: \"hard\" | \"soft\";\n  label: string;\n  content: React.ReactNode;\n};\n\nexport const PageFlipBook = forwardRef<\n  PageFlipBookHandle,\n  {\n    pages: FlipBookPage[];\n    mode: MotionMode;\n    className?: string;\n    initialPage?: number;\n    onPageChange?: (page: number) => void;\n  }\n>(function PageFlipBook({ pages, mode, className = \"\", initialPage = 0, onPageChange }, ref) {\n  const host = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const source = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const instance = useRef<PageFlipInstance | null>(null);\n  const [current, setCurrent] = useState(Math.min(initialPage, Math.max(0, pages.length - 1)));\n  const [orientation, setOrientation] = useState<\"portrait\" | \"landscape\">(\"landscape\");\n  const [failed, setFailed] = useState(false);\n  const signature = useMemo(() => pages.map((page) => page.id).join(\"|\"), [pages]);\n  const reduced = mode === \"reduced\";\n\n  const changePage = (page: number) => {\n    const next = Math.min(Math.max(0, page), pages.length - 1);\n    setCurrent(next);\n    onPageChange?.(next);\n  };\n\n  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({\n    next: () => (reduced ? changePage(current + 1) : instance.current?.flipNext(\"top\")),\n    previous: () => (reduced ? changePage(current - 1) : instance.current?.flipPrev(\"top\")),\n    turnTo: (page) => (reduced ? changePage(page) : instance.current?.turnToPage(page)),\n    flipTo: (page) => (reduced ? changePage(page) : instance.current?.flip(page, \"top\")),\n    currentPage: () => (reduced ? current : (instance.current?.getCurrentPageIndex() ?? current)),\n    pageCount: () => (reduced ? pages.length : (instance.current?.getPageCount() ?? pages.length)),\n    orientation: () => (reduced ? \"portrait\" : (instance.current?.getOrientation() ?? orientation)),\n  }));\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const hostElement = host.current;\n    const sourceElement = source.current;\n    if (reduced || !hostElement || !sourceElement) return;\n    let disposed = false;\n    void import(\"page-flip\")\n      .then(({ PageFlip }) => {\n        if (disposed) return;\n        const book = new PageFlip(hostElement, {\n          width: 560,\n          height: 760,\n          size: \"stretch\",\n          minWidth: 300,\n          maxWidth: 720,\n          minHeight: 420,\n          maxHeight: 960,\n          showCover: true,\n          usePortrait: true,\n          autoSize: true,\n          drawShadow: true,\n          maxShadowOpacity: 0.45,\n          flippingTime: mode === \"full\" ? 1100 : 620,\n          swipeDistance: 24,\n          mobileScrollSupport: true,\n          startPage: Math.min(initialPage, pages.length - 1),\n        });\n        const clones = Array.from(sourceElement.children).map((node) => node.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement);\n        book.loadFromHTML(clones);\n        book.on(\"flip\", (event) => {\n          const page = Number(event.data);\n          changePage(page);\n          window.requestAnimationFrame(() =>\n            hostElement.querySelector<HTMLElement>(`[data-page-index=\"${page}\"]`)?.focus({ preventScroll: true }),\n          );\n        });\n        book.on(\"changeOrientation\", (event) => setOrientation(event.data as \"portrait\" | \"landscape\"));\n        instance.current = book;\n        changeMountedMetric(\"pageFlip\", 1);\n      })\n      .catch(() => {\n        setFailed(true);\n        recordAssetFailure(\"page-flip\");\n      });\n    return () => {\n      disposed = true;\n      if (instance.current) {\n        instance.current.destroy();\n        instance.current = null;\n        changeMountedMetric(\"pageFlip\", -1);\n      }\n      hostElement.replaceChildren();\n    };\n    // The page-flip instance deliberately initializes once; updates are handled below.\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [reduced]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (reduced || !instance.current || !source.current) return;\n    const clones = Array.from(source.current.children).map((node) => node.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement);\n    instance.current.updateFromHtml(clones);\n    const safeCurrent = Math.min(instance.current.getCurrentPageIndex(), pages.length - 1);\n    if (safeCurrent !== instance.current.getCurrentPageIndex()) instance.current.turnToPage(safeCurrent);\n  }, [pages.length, reduced, signature]);\n\n  const pageNodes = pages.map((page, index) => (\n    <article\n      key={page.id}\n      className={`ft-page density-${page.density}`}\n      data-density={page.density}\n      data-page-index={index}\n      tabIndex={-1}\n      aria-label={page.label}\n    >\n      {page.content}\n    </article>\n  ));\n\n  if (reduced || failed) {\n    return (\n      <section\n        className={`page-flip-book reduced-page-book ${className}`}\n        data-pageflip-status={failed ? \"fallback\" : \"reduced\"}\n      >\n        <div className=\"reduced-page-stage\">{pages[current]?.content…24 tokens truncated…apshot } from \"@/domain/story\";\r\nimport { AnimationTestButton } from \"@/components/dev/AnimationTestButton\";\r\nimport { ArtifactInspection } from \"./workspace/ArtifactInspection\";\r\nimport { CompanionHeader } from \"./workspace/CompanionHeader\";\r\nimport { CompanionNavigation, MobileNavigation } from \"./workspace/CompanionNavigation\";\r\nimport { FinaleChamber } from \"./workspace/FinaleChamber\";\r\nimport { JournalWorkspace } from \"./workspace/JournalWorkspace\";\r\nimport { ObjectiveNote } from \"./workspace/ObjectiveNote\";\r\nimport { ShipsLog } from \"./workspace/ShipsLog\";\r\nimport { SideQuestLedger } from \"./workspace/SideQuestLedger\";\r\nimport { TreasureAltar } from \"./workspace/TreasureAltar\";\r\nimport { companionViews, type CompanionView } from \"./workspace/types\";\r\nimport { VoyageChart } from \"./workspace/VoyageChart\";\r\n\r\nconst sceneByEvent: Partial<Record<ClientProgressEvent[\"type\"], AnimationSceneName>> = {\r\n  CHAPTER_RELEASED: \"chapter-release\",\r\n  CHAPTER_SOLVED: \"mark-solved\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_AWARDED: \"artifact-award\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED: \"artifact-award\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_CONNECTED: \"artifact-connection\",\r\n  MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED: \"map-reveal\",\r\n  MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED: \"route-draw\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED: \"quest-discovery\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED: \"quest-discovery\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED: \"quest-complete\",\r\n  JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED: \"log-entry\",\r\n  PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED: \"log-entry\",\r\n  FINALE_TEASED: \"finale-tease\",\r\n  FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED: \"finale-requirement\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_PAUSED: \"pause\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_RESUMED: \"resume\",\r\n  STATE_REVERTED: \"undo\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nconst cueByEvent: Partial<Record<ClientProgressEvent[\"type\"], AudioCueName>> = {\r\n  CHAPTER_RELEASED: \"wax-crack\",\r\n  CHAPTER_SOLVED: \"stamp-impact\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_AWARDED: \"artifact-chime\",\r\n  MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED: \"compass-click\",\r\n  MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED: \"map-scratch\",\r\n  FINALE_TEASED: \"mechanism-hum\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_PAUSED: \"pause-wind-down\",\r\n  STATE_REVERTED: \"undo-reverse\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction deviceId() {\r\n  let id = localStorage.getItem(\"forever-device\");\r\n  if (!id) {\r\n    id = crypto.randomUUID();\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-device\", id);\r\n  }\r\n  return id;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction nextFrame() {\r\n  return new Promise<void>((resolve) => window.requestAnimationFrame(() => resolve()));\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function PlayerExperience({ initialSnapshot }: { initialSnapshot: PublicSnapshot }) {\r\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\r\n  const [snapshot, setSnapshot] = useState(initialSnapshot);\r\n  const [view, setView] = useState<CompanionView>(\"journal\");\r\n  const [opened, setOpened] = useState(false);\r\n  const [introVisible, setIntroVisible] = useState(false);\r\n  const [muted, setMuted] = useState(false);\r\n  const [volume, setVolumeState] = useState(0.4);\r\n  const [connection, setConnection] = useState<\"connecting\" | \"live\" | \"adrift\">(\"connecting\");\r\n  const [lastRelease, setLastRelease] = useState<ClientProgressEvent | null>(null);\r\n  const [activeEvent, setActiveEvent] = useState<ClientProgressEvent | null>(null);\r\n  const [selectedArtifact, setSelectedArtifact] = useState<string | null>(null);\r\n  const [inspectionOrigin, setInspectionOrigin] = useState<HTMLElement | null>(null);\r\n  const [objectiveOpen, setObjectiveOpen] = useState(false);\r\n  const [textScale, setTextScaleState] = useState(1);\r\n  const [texture, setTextureState] = useState(1);\r\n  const eventChain = useRef<Promise<void>>(Promise.resolve());\r\n  const seenEvents = useRef(new Set<string>());\r\n  const latestSequence = useRef(initialSnapshot.sequence);\r\n  const audio = useRef(new AudioCuePlayer());\r\n  const { director, snapshot: animation } = useAnimationDirector();\r\n  const { mode, cycle } = useMotionMode();\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const audioEngine = audio.current;\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n      const savedMuted = localStorage.getItem(\"forever-muted\") === \"true\";\r\n      const savedVolume = Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-volume\") ?? 0.4);\r\n      setMuted(savedMuted);\r\n      setVolumeState(savedVolume);\r\n      setTextScaleState(Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-text-scale\") ?? 1));\r\n      setTextureState(Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-texture\") ?? 1));\r\n      audioEngine.setMuted(savedMuted);\r\n      audioEngine.setVolume(savedVolume);\r\n    });\r\n    return () => audioEngine.close();\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  const refreshSnapshot = useCallback(async () => {\r\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/snapshot`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\r\n    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(\"The latest voyage state could not be loaded.\");\r\n    const next = (await response.json()) as PublicSnapshot;\r\n    latestSequence.current = Math.max(latestSequence.current, next.sequence);\r\n    setSnapshot(next);\r\n    return next;\r\n  }, [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug]);\r\n\r\n  const completeEvent = useCallback(\r\n    async (event: ClientProgressEvent) => {\r\n      try {\r\n        await refreshSnapshot();\r\n        if (!event.id.includes(\":replay:\")) {\r\n          await fetch(`/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/viewed`, {\r\n            method: \"POST\",\r\n            headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n            body: JSON.stringify({ eventId: event.id, deviceId: deviceId() }),\r\n          });\r\n        }\r\n      } catch {\r\n        setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n      }\r\n    },\r\n    [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug, refreshSnapshot],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const playEvent = useCallback(\r\n    async (event: ClientProgressEvent, replay = false) => {\r\n      if (!root.current) return;\r\n      const scene = sceneByEvent[event.type];\r\n      flushSync(() => {\r\n        if (event.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\") setLastRelease(event);\r\n        setActiveEvent(event);\r\n      });\r\n      const cue = cueByEvent[event.type];\r\n      if (cue) audio.current.play(cue);\r\n      try {\r\n        if (scene)\r\n          await director.play(scene, {\r\n            root: root.current,\r\n            display: event.payload as Record<string, string | number | boolean>,\r\n            queue: true,\r\n          });\r\n      } catch {\r\n        // Presentation failure must not prevent reconciliation with authoritative state.\r\n      }\r\n      if (!replay) await completeEvent(event);\r\n      setActiveEvent(null);\r\n    },\r\n    [completeEvent, director],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const source = new EventSource(\r\n      `/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/events?after=${latestSequence.current}`,\r\n    );\r\n    const offline = () => setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n    const online = () => {\r\n      setConnection(\"connecting\");\r\n      void refreshSnapshot()\r\n        .then(() => setConnection(\"live\"))\r\n        .catch(() => setConnection(\"adrift\"));\r\n    };\r\n    source.onopen = () => setConnection(\"live\");\r\n    source.onerror = () => setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n    source.addEventListener(\"progression\", (message) => {\r\n      const event = JSON.parse((message as MessageEvent).data) as ClientProgressEvent;\r\n      if (seenEvents.current.has(event.id)) return;\r\n      seenEvents.current.add(event.id);\r\n      latestSequence.current = Math.max(latestSequence.current, event.sequence);\r\n      eventChain.current = eventChain.current.then(() => playEvent(event)).catch(() => undefined);\r\n    });\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"offline\", offline);\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"online\", online);\r\n    return () => {\r\n      source.close();\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"offline\", offline);\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"online\", online);\r\n    };\r\n  }, [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug, playEvent, refreshSnapshot]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const readLocation = () => {\r\n      const requested = new URLSearchParams(location.search).get(\"section\") as CompanionView | null;\r\n      setView(requested && companionViews.some((item) => item.key === requested) ? requested : \"journal\");\r\n    };\r\n    readLocation();\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"popstate\", readLocation);\r\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"popstate\", readLocation);\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!opened) return;\r\n    const entries: Record<CompanionView, { contentType: string; contentKeys: string[] }> = {\r\n      journal: {\r\n        contentType: \"chapter\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.chapters.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => String(item.ordinal)),\r\n      },\r\n      chart: {\r\n        contentType: \"map\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.mapLocations.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      treasures: {\r\n        contentType: \"artifact\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.artifacts.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      quests: {\r\n        contentType: \"quest\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.sideQuests.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      log: { contentType: \"log\", contentKeys: snapshot.log.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key) },\r\n      finale: { contentType: \"finale\", contentKeys: snapshot.finale.unseen ? [snapshot.finale.state] : [] },\r\n    };\r\n    const entry = entries[view];\r\n    if (!entry.contentKeys.length) return;\r\n    const controller = new AbortController();\r\n    void fetch(`/api/player/${snapshot.campaign.slug}/viewed`, {\r\n      method: \"POST\",\r\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n      body: JSON.stringify(entry),\r\n      signal: controller.signal,\r\n    })\r\n      .then(() => refreshSnapshot())\r\n      .catch(() => undefined);\r\n    return () => controller.abort();\r\n  }, [opened, refreshSnapshot, snapshot, view]);\r\n\r\n  async function openJournal(forceFull = false) {\r\n    await audio.current.unlock();\r\n    setOpened(true);\r\n    setIntroVisible(true);\r\n    await nextFrame();\r\n    if (!root.current) return;\r\n\n---PLAYER EXPERIENCE PART 2---\r\n    const key = `forever-intro:${snapshot.campaign.slug}`;\r\n    const first = forceFull || sessionStorage.getItem(key) !== \"seen\";\r\n    sessionStorage.setItem(key, \"seen\");\r\n    audio.current.play(\"ocean-rise\");\r\n    try {\r\n      await director.play(first ? \"first-arrival\" : \"session-reentry\", { root: root.current, queue: false });\r\n    } catch {\r\n      /* local introduction only */\r\n    }\r\n    setIntroVisible(false);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function navigate(next: CompanionView) {\r\n    setView(next);\r\n    const url = new URL(location.href);\r\n    url.searchParams.set(\"section\", next);\r\n    history.pushState({}, \"\", url);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function toggleMute() {\r\n    setMuted((value) => {\r\n      const next = !value;\r\n      localStorage.setItem(\"forever-muted\", String(next));\r\n      audio.current.setMuted(next);\r\n      return next;\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  function setVolume(value: number) {\r\n    setVolumeState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-volume\", String(value));\r\n    audio.current.setVolume(value);\r\n  }\r\n  function setTextScale(value: number) {\r\n    setTextScaleState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-text-scale\", String(value));\r\n  }\r\n  function setTexture(value: number) {\r\n    setTextureState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-texture\", String(value));\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const payloadObjective =\r\n    activeEvent?.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\" &&\r\n    [\r\n      \"ink-objective\",\r\n      \"ink-riddle\",\r\n      \"map\",\r\n      \"active\",\r\n      \"content-readable\",\r\n      \"interaction-restored\",\r\n      \"scene-complete\",\r\n    ].includes(animation.label)\r\n      ? String(activeEvent.payload.objective ?? \"\")\r\n      : null;\r\n  const objective = payloadObjective || snapshot.chapter.objective || \"Await the captain's signal.\";\r\n  const inspectedArtifact = snapshot.artifacts.find((artifact) => artifact.key === selectedArtifact);\r\n  const animationSequence =\r\n    animation.scene === \"first-arrival\"\r\n      ? \"firstArrival\"\r\n      : animation.scene === \"session-reentry\"\r\n        ? \"reentry\"\r\n        : (animation.scene ?? \"dormant\");\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <main\r\n      ref={root}\r\n      className={`voyage-shell stage-${animation.label} view-${view}`}\r\n      data-cinematic-sequence={animationSequence}\r\n      data-motion-mode={mode}\r\n      style={{ \"--player-text-scale\": textScale, \"--texture-opacity\": texture } as React.CSSProperties}\r\n    >\r\n      <div className=\"ocean-depth\" data-scene-part=\"workspace-light\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"sky\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"horizon\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"ocean\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"fog-back\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"fog-front\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div className=\"player-lantern\" data-scene-part=\"lantern\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <i />\r\n        <b />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <CompanionHeader\r\n        connection={connection}\r\n        muted={muted}\r\n        volume={volume}\r\n        mode={mode}\r\n        textScale={textScale}\r\n        texture={texture}\r\n        canReplay={Boolean(lastRelease)}\r\n        toggleMute={toggleMute}\r\n        setVolume={setVolume}\r\n        cycleMotion={cycle}\r\n        setTextScale={setTextScale}\r\n        setTexture={setTexture}\r\n        replay={() =>\r\n          lastRelease && void playEvent({ ...lastRelease, id: `${lastRelease.id}:replay:${Date.now()}` }, true)\r\n        }\r\n      />\r\n      <CompanionNavigation view={view} unseen={snapshot.unseen} navigate={navigate} />\r\n      {!opened && (\r\n        <div className=\"journal-opening\">\r\n          <button className=\"wax-open\" onClick={() => void openJournal()}>\r\n            <span>F</span>\r\n            <strong>Open the journal</strong>\r\n            <small>Sound begins only after you choose</small>\r\n          </button>\r\n        </div>\r\n      )}\r\n      {opened && (\r\n        <div className=\"physical-workspace\">\r\n          <AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\" initial={false}>\r\n            <motion.div\r\n              key={view}\r\n              className=\"section-transition\"\r\n              variants={sectionVariants(mode)}\r\n              initial=\"initial\"\r\n              animate=\"enter\"\r\n              exit=\"exit\"\r\n              onAnimationComplete={(definition) => {\r\n                if (definition === \"enter\") {\r\n                  document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(\"[data-section-heading]\")?.focus({ preventScroll: true });\r\n                }\r\n              }}\r\n              data-animation-owner=\"motion\"\r\n            >\r\n              {view === \"journal\" && <JournalWorkspace snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} activeEvent={activeEvent} />}\r\n              {view === \"chart\" && <VoyageChart snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} />}\r\n              {view === \"treasures\" && (\r\n                <TreasureAltar\r\n                  snapshot={snapshot}\r\n                  inspect={(key, element) => {\r\n                    setInspectionOrigin(element);\r\n                    setSelectedArtifact(key);\r\n                  }}\r\n                />\r\n              )}\r\n              {view === \"quests\" && <SideQuestLedger snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} />}\r\n              {view === \"log\" && <ShipsLog snapshot={snapshot} navigate={navigate} />}\r\n              {view === \"finale\" && <FinaleChamber snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} />}\r\n            </motion.div>\r\n          </AnimatePresence>\r\n        </div>\r\n      )}\r\n      {opened && (\r\n        <ObjectiveNote\r\n          objective={objective}\r\n          chapter={snapshot.chapter.ordinal}\r\n          title={snapshot.chapter.title}\r\n          hintCount={snapshot.chapter.hints?.length ?? 0}\r\n          expanded={objectiveOpen}\r\n          setExpanded={setObjectiveOpen}\r\n          returnToClue={() => navigate(\"journal\")}\r\n        />\r\n      )}\r\n      <MobileNavigation view={view} unseen={snapshot.unseen} navigate={navigate} />\r\n      <AnimatePresence>\r\n        {inspectedArtifact && (\r\n          <ArtifactInspection\r\n            artifact={inspectedArtifact}\r\n            close={() => setSelectedArtifact(null)}\r\n            restoreFocus={inspectionOrigin}\r\n          />\r\n        )}\r\n      </AnimatePresence>\r\n      {introVisible && (\r\n        <div className={`voyage-introduction intro-${animationSequence}`} role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\">\r\n          <div className=\"intro-horizon\" data-scene-part=\"horizon\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\" />\r\n          <div \"../core/visibility\";\nimport { gsap } from \"../core/gsap-client\";\nimport { getSceneDefinition } from \"./scene-registry\";\n\nconst initialSnapshot = (mode: MotionMode): DirectorSnapshot => ({\n  isPlaying: false,\n  isPaused: false,\n  scene: null,\n  label: \"idle\",\n  progress: 0,\n  speed: 1,\n  mode,\n  phase: \"idle\",\n  queueDepth: 0,\n  error: null,\n});\n\nexport class AnimationDirector {\n  private snapshot: DirectorSnapshot;\n  private listeners = new Set<() => void>();\n  private currentTimeline: SceneTimeline | null = null;\n  private currentResolve: (() => void) | null = null;\n  private cleanups: Array<() => void> = [];\n  private tail: Promise<unknown> = Promise.resolve();\n  private cancelled = false;\n  private skipRequested = false;\n  private userPaused = false;\n  private visibilityCleanup: () => void;\n  private destroyed = false;\n  private cancelWait: (() => void) | null = null;\n\n  constructor(mode: MotionMode = \"full\") {\n    this.snapshot = initialSnapshot(mode);\n    this.visibilityCleanup = observeDocumentVisibility((visible) => {\n      if (!this.currentTimeline) return;\n      if (!visible) this.currentTimeline.pause();\n      else if (!this.userPaused && this.snapshot.phase !== \"await-server\") this.currentTimeline.resume();\n    });\n  }\n\n  subscribe = (listener: () => void) => {\n    this.listeners.add(listener);\n    return () => this.listeners.delete(listener);\n  };\n\n  getSnapshot = () => this.snapshot;\n\n  private update(patch: Partial<DirectorSnapshot>) {\n    this.snapshot = { ...this.snapshot, ...patch };\n    this.listeners.forEach((listener) => listener());\n  }\n\n  setMode(mode: MotionMode) {\n    this.update({ mode });\n  }\n\n  setSpeed(multiplier: number) {\n    const speed = Math.min(2, Math.max(0.25, multiplier));\n    this.currentTimeline?.timeScale(speed);\n    this.update({ speed });\n  }\n\n  pause() {\n    this.userPaused = true;\n    this.currentTimeline?.pause();\n    this.update({ isPaused: true });\n  }\n\n  resume() {\n    this.userPaused = false;\n    this.currentTimeline?.resume();\n    this.update({ isPaused: false });\n  }\n\n  seek(labelOrProgress: string | number) {\n    const timeline = this.currentTimeline;\n    if (!timeline) return;\n    if (typeof labelOrProgress === \"number\") timeline.progress(Math.max(0, Math.min(1, labelOrProgress)));\n    else if (timeline.labels[labelOrProgress] !== undefined) timeline.seek(labelOrProgress);\n    this.update({\n      progress: timeline.progress(),\n      label: typeof labelOrProgress === \"string\" ? labelOrProgress : this.snapshot.label,\n    });\n  }\n\n  reverse() {\n    const scene = this.snapshot.scene;\n    if (!scene || !getSceneDefinition(scene).reversible || !this.currentTimeline) return false;\n    this.currentTimeline.reverse();\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  skip() {\n    this.skipRequested = true;\n    if (!this.currentTimeline) return;\n    this.currentTimeline.progress(1, false);\n  }\n\n  cancel(reason = \"cancelled\") {\n    this.cancelled = true;\n    this.cancelWait?.();\n    this.currentTimeline?.kill();\n    this.currentResolve?.();\n    this.update({ error: reason, isPlaying: false, isPaused: false, phase: \"idle\", label: \"cancelled\" });\n  }\n\n  kill() {\n    this.destroyed = true;\n    this.cancel(\"director-killed\");\n    this.visibilityCleanup();\n    this.finishCleanup();\n    this.listeners.clear();\n  }\n\n  play<T = void>(name: AnimationSceneName, options: PlaySceneOptions<T>): Promise<T | undefined> {\n    if (this.destroyed) return Promise.reject(new AnimationCancelledError(\"Animation director has been killed.\"));\n    if (options.queue === false && (this.snapshot.isPlaying || this.snapshot.queueDepth > 0)) {\n      return Promise.reject(new Error(\"A conflicting cinematic scene is already active.\"));\n    }\n    this.update({ queueDepth: this.snapshot.queueDepth + 1 });\n    const scheduled = this.tail\n      .catch(() => undefined)\n      .then(async () => {\n        this.update({ queueDepth: Math.max(0, this.snapshot.queueDepth - 1) });\n        return this.execute(name, options);\n      });\n    this.tail = scheduled.catch(() => undefined);\n    return scheduled;\n  }\n\n  private async execute<T>(name: AnimationSceneName, options: PlaySceneOptions<T>): Promise<T | undefined> {\n    const definition = getSceneDefinition(name);\n    this.cancelled = false;\n    this.skipRequested = false;\n    this.userPaused = false;\n    this.update({\n      isPlaying: true,\n      isPaused: false,\n      scene: name,\n      label: \"scene-start\",\n      progress: 0,\n      phase: \"opening\",\n      error: null,\n    });\n    const operation = options.operation?.().then(\n      (value) => ({ ok: true as const, value }),\n      (error: unknown) => ({ ok: false as const, error }),\n    );\n    let operationSettled = false;\n    void operation?.then(() => (operationSettled = true));\n\n    const cleanupOwnership = claimSceneTargets(options.root, \"[data-gsap-owned]\", \"gsap\", [\n      \"transform\",\n      \"opacity\",\n      \"filter\",\n      \"clip-path\",\n      \"geometry\",\n    ]);\n    this.cleanups.push(cleanupOwnership);\n    const sceneContext: SceneBuildContext = {\n      root: options.root,\n      mode: this.snapshot.mode,\n      sceneName: name,\n      display: options.display ?? {},\n      emitLabel: (label) => this.update({ label }),\n      addCleanup: (cleanup) => this.cleanups.push(cleanup),\n    };\n    let opening!: SceneTimeline;\n    let success!: SceneTimeline;\n    let failure: SceneTimeline | undefined;\n    let idle: SceneTimeline | undefined;\n    const gsapContext = gsap.context(() => {\n      opening = definition.buildOpening(sceneContext);\n      success = definition.buildSuccess(sceneContext);\n      failure = definition.buildFailure?.(sceneContext);\n      idle = definition.buildIdle?.(sceneContext);\n    }, options.root);\n    this.cleanups.push(() => gsapContext.revert());\n\n    try {\n      await this.runTimeline(opening, \"opening\");\n      this.throwIfCancelled();\n      let result: T | undefined;\n      if (operation) {\n        this.update({ phase: \"await-server\", label: \"await-server\", progress: 1 });\n        if (!operationSettled && idle) this.startIdle(idle);\n        const cancelled = new Promise<{ cancelled: true }>((resolve) => {\n          this.cancelWait = () => resolve({ cancelled: true });\n        });\n        const settled = await Promise.race([operation, cancelled]);\n        this.cancelWait = null;\n        this.stopIdle();\n        this.throwIfCancelled();\n        if (\"cancelled\" in settled) throw new AnimationCancelledError();\n        if (!settled.ok) {\n          this.update({ phase: \"failure\", label: \"failure-branch\" });\n          if (failure) await this.runTimeline(failure, \"failure\");\n          throw settled.error;\n        }\n        result = settled.value;\n      }\n      this.update({ phase: \"success\", label: \"success-branch\", progress: 0 });\n      if (this.skipRequested) success.progress(1, true);\n      else await this.runTimeline(success, \"success\");\n      this.throwIfCancelled();\n      return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n      if (!(error instanceof AnimationCancelledError)) {\n        this.update({ error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"Animation failed\" });\n this.finishCleanup();\n      this.update({ isPlaying: false, isPaused: false, scene: null, label: \"idle\", progress: 0, phase: \"idle\" });\n    }\n  }\n\n  private runTimeline(timeline: SceneTimeline, phase: DirectorSnapshot[\"phase\"]) {\n    return new Promise<void>((resolve) => {\n      this.currentTimeline?.kill();\n      this.currentTimeline = timeline;\n      changeMountedMetric(\"gsap\", 1);\n      let counted = true;\n      const finish = () => {\n        if (counted) changeMountedMetric(\"gsap\", -1);\n        counted = false;\n        this.currentResolve = null;\n        resolve();\n      };\n      this.currentResolve = finish;\n      timeline.eventCallback(\"onUpdate\", () => this.update({ progress: timeline.progress() }));\n      timeline.eventCallback(\"onComplete\", finish);\n      timeline.eventCallback(\"onReverseComplete\", finish);\n      timeline.timeScale(this.snapshot.speed);\n      this.update({ phase, progress: timeline.progress() });\n      timeline.play(0);\n    });\n  }\n\n  private startIdle(timeline: SceneTimeline) {\n    this.currentTimeline = timeline;\n    timeline.timeScale(this.snapshot.speed).play(0);\n  }\n\n  private stopIdle() {\n    this.currentTimeline?.kill();\n    this.currentTimeline = null;\n  }\n\n  private throwIfCancelled() {\n    if (this.cancelled) throw new AnimationCancelledError();\n  }\n\n  private finishCleanup() {\n    this.cleanups\n      .splice(0)\n      .reverse()\n      .forEach((cleanup) => {\n        try {\n          cleanup();\n        } catch {\n          // Cleanup must be best-effort and must not mask an authoritative result.\n        }\n      });\n  }\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

journalOpeningPhases[index + 1] : null;\n+}\n+\n+export function isJournalInteractive(phase: JournalOpeningPhase) {\n+  return phase === \"JOURNAL_READY\";\n+}\n+\n+function abortError() {\n+  return new DOMException(\"The journal opening was interrupted.\", \"AbortError\");\n+}\n+\n+function nextPaint(signal: AbortSignal) {\n+  return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {\n+    if (signal.aborted) {\n+      reject(abortError());\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    let secondFrame = 0;\n+    const firstFrame = requestAnimationFrame(() => {\n+      secondFrame = requestAnimationFrame(() => {\n+        signal.removeEventListener(\"abort\", onAbort);\n+        resolve();\n+      });\n+    });\n+    const onAbort = () => {\n+      cancelAnimationFrame(firstFrame);\n+      cancelAnimationFrame(secondFrame);\n+      reject(abortError());\n+    };\n+    signal.addEventListener(\"abort\", onAbort, { once: true });\n+  });\n+}\n+\n+/** Waits for the CSS work owned by one physical phase instead of chaining timing guesses. */\n+export async function waitForJournalPhase(\n+  root: HTMLElement,\n+  phase: JournalOpeningPhase,\n+  mode: MotionMode,\n+  signal: AbortSignal,\n+) {\n+  await nextPaint(signal);\n+  if (signal.aborted) throw abortError();\n+  const selectors = phaseTargets[phase] ?? [];\n+  const animations = selectors.flatMap((selector) => {\n+    const actor = root.querySelector<HTMLElement>(selector);\n+    return actor?.getAnimations({ subtree: true }) ?? [];\n+  });\n+  const uniqueAnimations = [...new Set(animations)];\n+  if (!uniqueAnimations.length || mode === \"reduced\") {\n+    await nextPaint(signal);\n+    return;\n+  }\n+  await Promise.allSettled(uniqueAnimations.map((animation) => animation.finished));\n+  if (signal.aborted) throw abortError();\n+}\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion\\src\\animation\\journal\\opening-machine.test.ts\n+import { describe, expect, it } from \"vitest\";\n+import {\n+  isJournalInteractive,\n+  journalOpeningPhases,\n+  nextJournalOpeningPhase,\n+} from \"./opening-machine\";\n+\n+describe(\"journal opening state machine\", () => {\n+  it(\"advances through the complete physical sequence in one direction\", () => {\n+    const visited = [journalOpeningPhases[0]];\n+    let next = nextJournalOpeningPhase(visited[0]);\n+    while (next) {\n+      visited.push(next);\n+      next = nextJournalOpeningPhase(next);\n+    }\n+    expect(visited).toEqual(journalOpeningPhases);\n+  });\n+\n+  it(\"does not enable journal interaction before the book settles\", () => {\n+    expect(journalOpeningPhases.slice(0, -1).every((phase) => !isJournalInteractive(phase))).toBe(true);\n+    expect(isJournalInteractive(\"JOURNAL_READY\")).toBe(true);\n+    expect(nextJournalOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\")).toBeNull();\n+  });\n+});\n*** End Patch";

Prefer this method of controlling Chrome over alternatives (such as Computer Use) unless the user explicitly mentions an alternative.\\n\\nRun JavaScript in a persistent Node-backed kernel with top-level await. This is the JavaScript execution tool for the `node_repl` MCP server; use it whenever instructions say to use `node_repl`, the Node REPL MCP, or run Node REPL code. If `timeout_ms` is omitted, execution times out after 30000 ms (30 seconds); pass a larger `timeout_ms` for slow browser automation or other long-running operations. Use `nodeRepl.cwd`, `nodeRepl.homeDir`, and `nodeRepl.tmpDir` to inspect host paths. Use `nodeRepl.requestMeta` to inspect the current MCP request `_meta` object during a tool call. Use `nodeRepl.setResponseMeta(meta)` to attach top-level MCP result `_meta`; repeated calls shallow-merge object keys for the current tool call. Use `nodeRepl.write(value)` to add output without a newline. Strings are unchanged; other values use console-style formatting, including BigInt and circular objects. Prefer it over `console.log(...)` for final output; `console.log(...)` remains useful for debugging or multiple values. Use `await nodeRepl.emitImage(imageLike)` to return images; each call adds one image to the outer tool result, so call it multiple times to emit multiple images. Supported image inputs are a data URL, inferred PNG/JPEG/WebP bytes, or `{ bytes, mimeType }`. Saved references to `nodeRepl.write(...)` and `nodeRepl.emitImage(...)` stay reusable across calls, but async callbacks that fire after a call finishes still fail because no exec is active. Top-level bindings persist across calls until `js_reset`. If a call throws, prior bindings remain available and bindings that finished initializing before the throw often remain reusable. For reusable names that may be assigned again later, prefer top-level `var name = ...`; `var` can be redeclared across calls. If you hit `SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared`, reuse the existing binding if possible, reassign it only if it was declared with `let` or `var`, or pick a new name instead of resetting immediately; a previous `const x` cannot be changed into `var x`. Use a short `{ ... }` block only for temporary scratch names, and do not wrap an entire call in block scope if you want those names reusable later. Use dynamic imports like `await import(\\\"playwright\\\")`, `await import(\\\"pkg\\\")`, or `await import(\\\"./file.js\\\")`; top-level static `import` is not supported. Import packages by package name after installing them into a directory added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, `NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, or the working directory. Do not import package entrypoints by filesystem path such as `./node_modules/playwright/index.mjs`. Imported local files must be ESM `.js` or `.mjs` files and run in the context chosen at their dynamic-import boundary, so they can also use `nodeRepl.*`, the captured `console`, and `import.meta` helpers. Bare imports from model code and local files resolve from the REPL-wide search roots (`NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, then directories later added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, then cwd); dependencies of trusted ESM packages use Node's package-relative lookup. Imported local files may statically import other local `.js` / `.mjs` files, available packages, and allowed Node builtins. `import.meta.resolve()` returns importable strings such as `file://...`, bare package names, and `node:...` specifiers. Local file modules reload between execs; trusted package entrypoints retain singleton identity. `node:` builtins are generally available via dynamic import, but model code cannot import `process` / `node:process` because the current Rust-server-to-Node-child transport runs over stdio and raw process streams can corrupt it. Trusted modules that import or reference `process` receive only a frozen metadata-only process shim with `arch`, `cwd()`, `env`, `pid`, and `platform`.
